quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after CIGAR canonicalization
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because canonicalizing CIGAR match operators (= vs M) does not change the semantics or validity of the alignment.

evidence.quote: CIGAR operations M (alignment match) and = (sequence match) / X (sequence mismatch) are interchangeable in terms of validity; canonicalization preserves validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after removing zero-length CIGAR ops
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because zero-length CIGAR operations are semantically meaningless and their removal does not affect validity.

evidence.quote: Zero-length CIGAR operations are redundant and can be removed without changing the alignment semantics or violating SAM validity rules.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after normalizing sequence case
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because SEQ field case (upper/lower) does not affect SAM validity.

evidence.quote: SAM SEQ field accepts both upper and lower case nucleotide characters; case normalization preserves validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after normalizing unmapped record fields
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because normalizing fields of unmapped reads according to spec conventions preserves validity.

evidence.quote: For unmapped reads, certain fields (RNAME, POS, MAPQ, CIGAR) have conventional values; normalizing to these conventions preserves validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after canonicalizing RNEXT alias
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because RNEXT '=' and '==' are equivalent aliases for the same reference; canonicalizing between them preserves validity.

evidence.quote: In SAM, RNEXT field uses '=' as an alias for the same reference as RNAME; canonicalizing between '=' and '==' representations preserves validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after stripping mate flags if unpaired
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because mate-pair flags on unpaired reads are meaningless and stripping them preserves validity.

evidence.quote: Flags 0x40 (first in pair) and 0x80 (second in pair) are only meaningful when 0x1 (paired) is set; stripping them from unpaired reads preserves validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after reordering header records
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because the SAM spec does not mandate a specific order for header record types beyond conventional ordering.

evidence.quote: SAM header records (@HD, @SQ, @RG, @PG, etc.) have conventional but not strictly enforced ordering; reordering preserves validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Valid file remains valid after permuting optional tag fields
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be valid (not rejected), because the order of optional tags in a SAM record is not semantically significant.

evidence.quote: Optional tags in SAM records can appear in any order; permuting them does not affect validity.

quarantine.mr_name: Invalid file with bad flag bits remains rejected after round-trip
preconditions: Input SAM file contains records with invalid flag bit combinations
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be rejected (or remain invalid), because violating flag bit exclusivity creates an invalid record that should be consistently rejected.

evidence.quote: Certain SAM flag bit combinations are mutually exclusive (e.g., 0x4 unmapped and 0x2 mapped); violating this exclusivity creates an invalid record.

quarantine.mr_name: Invalid file with bad optional tag type remains rejected after round-trip
preconditions: Input SAM file contains records with invalid optional tag type characters
quarantine.oracle: The output file must also be rejected (or remain invalid), because violating the optional tag type character convention creates an invalid record that should be consistently rejected.

evidence.quote: Optional tag type characters (A, i, f, Z, H, B) are defined by the SAM spec; using invalid type characters creates an invalid record.
